steps.title: Find the x-intercept and y-intercept
steps.content:
To find the x-intercept (where $y=0$), substitute $y=0$ into $9x+4y=-37$:
$$9x + 4(0) = -37$$
$$9x = -37$$
$$x = -\frac{37}{9}$$
So $a = -\frac{37}{9}$.

To find the y-intercept (where $x=0$), substitute $x=0$:
$$9(0) + 4y = -37$$
$$4y = -37$$
$$y = -\frac{37}{4}$$
So $b = -\frac{37}{4}$.

steps.title: Compute the ratio b/a
steps.content:
Now calculate $\frac{b}{a}$:
$$\frac{b}{a} = \frac{-37/4}{-37/9}$$
Multiply by the reciprocal:
$$= \frac{-37}{4} \cdot \frac{9}{-37}$$
Cancel the common factor $-37$:
$$= \frac{9}{4}$$
That confirms D is correct.
